asset_type: Product-led TaraHome technical blog cover for an IKEA DIRIGERA, Matter, Thread, and Home Assistant compatibility guide
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover background for a product-led Matter and Thread compatibility article
Primary request: Create a clean, believable warm residential utility scene designed to receive two real smart-home product cutouts later. Show a broad low oak console shelf in the foreground with two clearly separate empty placement areas, subtle natural linen wall texture, and a softly out-of-focus modern living room beyond. Include one restrained dark-green ceramic object or plant pot in the far background as a Tara accent, not on the product shelf.
Style/medium: premium natural editorial interior photography, realistic materials and mild everyday imperfection
Composition/framing: landscape 3:2, eye-level three-quarter view, uncluttered oak shelf spanning the lower third, balanced empty space for two small tabletop devices, card-crop-safe focal area centered
Lighting/mood: soft late-morning window light, warm but neutral, gentle grounded shadows, calm practical home
Materials/textures: real oak grain, woven linen wall covering, matte plaster, natural plant leaves
Constraints: background scene only; absolutely no electronics, hubs, radios, phones, cables, smart-home devices, screens, text, logos, labels, diagrams, arrows, connection paths, network lines, UI, charts, translucent panels, inset frames, floating cards, progress bars, watermarks, or poster styling
Avoid: beige AI device diagrams, futuristic lab scenes, glossy CGI, symmetrical showroom staging, empty white studio, fake hardware, heavy dark-green tint
composite_note: Official IKEA DIRIGERA hub and BILRESA remote product renders were isolated from flat backgrounds, scaled proportionally, placed without redesign, and given restrained contact shadows.
